JavaScript Deep Dive: The Weird Parts of JS
The Weird Parts of JS (No.1): Syntax Parsers, Execution Contexts, and Lexical Environments

Kicking off a whole series on the weird parts of JavaScript — the language people misunderstand more than any other. In this first post I take notes on three foundational ideas: the syntax parser, the lexical environment, and the execution context. They sound scary, but they're not. Let's untangle them.
